COMPARISON: ZERO TRUST SECURITY vs. DEFENSE IN DEPTH
================================================================================

1. OVERVIEW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ZERO TRUST SECURITY
    A security model that operates on the principle of "never trust, always
    verify." It assumes that threats exist both outside and inside the
    traditional network perimeter, and therefore no user, device, or system
    should be trusted by default вҖ” even if they are already inside the network.

DEFENSE IN DEPTH
    A layered security strategy that employs multiple, overlapping defensive
    controls to protect systems and data. The idea is that if one layer fails,
    the next layer will catch the threat. It draws inspiration from military
    defense strategies where multiple lines of defense slow and deter attackers.


2. CORE PRINCIPLES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ZERO TRUST PRINCIPLES:
    вҖў Verify Explicitly вҖ” Authenticate and authorize based on all available
      data points (identity, location, device health, service, classification).
    вҖў Least Privilege Access вҖ” Grant only the minimum access necessary, and
      only for the duration required (Just-In-Time access).
    вҖў Assume Breach вҖ” Operate as if the network is already compromised.
      Segment access, encrypt everywhere, and use continuous monitoring.
    вҖў Micro-Segmentation вҖ” Divide the network into small, isolated zones;
      lateral movement requires re-authentication at each boundary.
    вҖў Continuous Verification вҖ” Trust is never permanent; it is constantly
      re-evaluated based on behavior, context, and risk signals.

DEFENSE IN DEPTH PRINCIPLES:
    вҖў Layered Controls вҖ” Deploy multiple security controls across physical,
      technical, and administrative domains.
    вҖў No Single Point of Failure вҖ” Redundancy ensures that the failure of
      one control does not result in a complete breach.
    вҖў Diversity of Defenses вҖ” Use different types of controls (firewalls,
      IDS/IPS, encryption, access controls, training) so that an attacker
      must bypass varied obstacles.
    вҖў Perimeter-First Mindset вҖ” Traditionally emphasizes a strong outer
      boundary (firewalls, VPNs) with additional internal layers.
    вҖў Depth Across Domains вҖ” Covers physical security, network security,
      endpoint security, application security, data security, and
      user awareness/training.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
